title: MRD Geometry Falsification Matrix v0
falsification_rule: Every geometry node must name what would weaken, split, merge, or block it before hypothesis generation is allowed. These are research-structure tests only.

matrix_rows.label: MRD trajectory split
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: measurement movement under assay, specimen, threshold, timing, and cohort constraints

evidence_that_would_weaken: a new source shows trajectory labels are assay artifacts without aligned method context
paired samples show discordant movement across assay frames
evidence_that_would_split: different MRD methods produce distinct trajectory categories that should not share one edge
bone marrow and non-bone-marrow contexts require separate measurement trajectories
evidence_that_would_merge: independent sources align assay, threshold, specimen, timing, and response labels closely enough for a shared source-bounded trajectory state
evidence_that_would_block: relapse risk, monitoring, treatment, or patient-specific MRD interpretation is inferred from trajectory labels

matrix_rows.label: Residual transcriptional adaptation
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: residual malignant plasma-cell state under method, exposure, and timing constraints

evidence_that_would_weaken: state labels are not reproducible across source-defined methods or therapy exposures
signals collapse into batch, sampling, or annotation effects
evidence_that_would_split: adaptive state, pathway context, and therapy-exposure context require separate typed subnodes
single-cell state and bulk pathway evidence diverge
evidence_that_would_merge: independent sources align residual-state labels, therapy exposure, and sampling frame without clinical overread
evidence_that_would_block: state labels are used as treatment selectors, target selectors, prognosis tools, or monitoring rules

matrix_rows.label: Metabolic resistance state
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: metabolic pathway context as a residual-state branch, not an intervention claim

evidence_that_would_weaken: metabolic findings are review-only or source-general and cannot be tied to residual disease context
primary sources contradict direction or cell-state specificity
evidence_that_would_split: fatty-acid oxidation, lipid uptake, adipocyte interaction, and metabolic stress require separate typed contexts
evidence_that_would_merge: primary residual-state sources align metabolic markers, methods, and sampling frames
evidence_that_would_block: metabolic context becomes supplement guidance, biomarker use, target selection, prognosis, or treatment advice

matrix_rows.label: NF-kB selective state
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: NF-kB pathway context as a source-bounded residual-state branch

evidence_that_would_weaken: NF-kB signal is broad pathway review context without selective residual-cell support
source-specific pathway labels conflict across methods
evidence_that_would_split: canonical/noncanonical pathway context or malignant/TME context requires separation
evidence_that_would_merge: primary residual-cell sources align NF-kB state, assay method, and response context
evidence_that_would_block: NF-kB context becomes target selection, pathway ranking, drug guidance, prognosis, or actionability

matrix_rows.label: Genetic and nongenetic coupling
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: provisional clone-state coupling edge, not causality or dominance

evidence_that_would_weaken: clone identity and transcriptional state fail to co-vary in aligned source frames
coupling appears method-specific or sampling-specific
evidence_that_would_split: genetic clone architecture, transcriptional state, and therapy exposure require independent transition tracks
evidence_that_would_merge: paired sources align clone architecture and state movement while preserving correlation language
evidence_that_would_block: coupling becomes causal ranking, genetic-versus-nongenetic dominance, patient-specific resistance attribution, or treatment guidance

matrix_rows.label: Tumor microenvironment shelter interactions
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: microenvironment support context with explicit inference-method limits

evidence_that_would_weaken: cell-interaction inference is review-level or computational-only without source support
TME signal fails to map to malignant plasma-cell context
evidence_that_would_split: immune, stromal, adipocyte, cytokine, and spatial contexts require separate support layers
evidence_that_would_merge: primary and review sources align interaction context without implying patient-specific mechanism
evidence_that_would_block: TME context becomes microenvironment-targeting recommendation, mechanism ranking, or patient-specific biology

matrix_rows.label: Subclone diversity
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: clone-architecture diversity under genomic method and sampling constraints

evidence_that_would_weaken: subclone diversity differs by platform or sampling frame enough to block comparison
genomic heterogeneity is not linked to the intended disease context
evidence_that_would_split: newly diagnosed, residual, relapse, and first-relapse clone architecture require separate contexts
evidence_that_would_merge: sources align clone/subclone method, sampling, disease timing, and uncertainty
evidence_that_would_block: subclone diversity becomes prognosis, relapse risk, treatment selection, or mechanism ranking

matrix_rows.label: Single-cell translation gap
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: technology-readiness and implementation context, separate from biology

evidence_that_would_weaken: a source claims clinical readiness without platform, workflow, validation, or implementation context
translation barriers are too generic for multiple myeloma context
evidence_that_would_split: cost, standardization, data complexity, ethics, workflow, and validation require separate readiness blockers
evidence_that_would_merge: multiple sources identify the same implementation blocker class with compatible context
evidence_that_would_block: translation context becomes test-ordering guidance, platform recommendation, monitoring guidance, or treatment guidance

matrix_rows.label: T-cell therapy pressure
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: immune-redirection and therapy-exposure context, not product or sequence advice

evidence_that_would_weaken: immune-state association is not reproducible across product, timing, or response contexts
therapy-pressure signal cannot be separated from disease burden or sampling frame
evidence_that_would_split: BCMA, non-BCMA, CAR T, bispecific, persistence, exhaustion, and niche contexts require separate support layers
evidence_that_would_merge: sources align therapy class, target class, response timing, and immune-state language
evidence_that_would_block: therapy pressure becomes product comparison, target selection, treatment sequencing, trial fit, or prognosis

matrix_rows.label: Unmet high-risk context
matrix_rows.hypothesis_scope: high-risk disease-context modifier, not personal risk or treatment fit

evidence_that_would_weaken: high-risk definitions differ enough that sources cannot be compared
risk context is not tied to source-defined disease timing or genomic method
evidence_that_would_split: genomic, functional, extramedullary, plasma-cell leukemia, early-relapse, and treatment-refractory contexts require separate modifiers
evidence_that_would_merge: sources align high-risk definition, disease timing, genomic method, and cohort frame
evidence_that_would_block: high-risk context becomes personal prognosis, eligibility, treatment selection, transplant fit, trial fit, or monitoring guidance

review_notes: Created for Phase 6 falsification and hypothesis generation.
Every current MRD geometry bucket has a falsification row.
The matrix does not upgrade any claim beyond research-map navigation.
